I have a solution with 5 MSTest test projects all using VSTest (MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0,
MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0). We use Azure DevOps
with the VSTest@3 task, TRX reporting, and code coverage. We're on .NET 8 SDK.
I want a complete migration plan to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.

arguments.file_text:
# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Current State
- 5 MSTest test projects
- MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0 / MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0 / Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
- Azure DevOps CI with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage
- .NET 8 SDK

## Approach
Migrate each test project to the MTP runner built into MSTest 3.8+, replace the
VSTest@3 pipeline task with `dotnet test`, and wire up TRX + code-coverage via
MTP extension packages. Migrate one project first as a pilot, then roll out to the
remaining four.

---

## Phase 1 — Project-file changes (per test project)

### 1a. Remove VSTest-era packages
Remove from each `.csproj`:
```xml
<!-- DELETE these three PackageReferences -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
```
Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` (or upgrade it — see 1b).

### 1b. Enable the MTP runner
Add two properties and the runner meta-package:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <!-- Optional: keeps dotnet test working alongside direct exe invocation -->
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```
`EnableMSTestRunner` bundles the MTP entry point into the test project.
`OutputType` must be `Exe` so the project compiles to a self-contained test host.

### 1c. Add MTP extension packages for TRX + coverage
```xml
<!-- TRX report generation (replaces VSTest's built-in TRX) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage via Microsoft's native collector -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```
> Version numbers above are latest stable as of May 2025 — check NuGet for updates.

### 1d. (Alternative) Use the MSTest SDK for a leaner csproj
Instead of steps 1a-1c you can switch the project SDK, which handles everything
automatically:
```xml
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```
The `MSTest.Sdk` already sets `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType`, and pulls in
the runner, framework, TRX, and code-coverage extension packages. This is the
cleanest option if your csproj files are straightforward.

---

## Phase 2 — Local verification

### 2a. Build & run locally
```bash
dotnet build MyTests.csproj
dotnet test MyTests.csproj          # uses MTP via dotnet-test bridge
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests          # direct exe invocation (also works)
```

### 2b. Verify TRX output
```bash
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --report-trx
# TRX file written to TestResults/ by default
```

### 2c. Verify code coverage
```bash
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
# Produces coverage.cobertura.xml in TestResults/
```

### 2d. Check for incompatible features
| Feature | VSTest | MTP |
|---|---|---|
| `[DataRow]`, `[DynamicData]`, `[DataSource]` | ✅ | ✅ |
| `.runsettings` | Passed via `--settings` | Passed via `--settings` (same) |
| Ordered tests (`.orderedtest`) | ✅ | ❌ Not supported — refactor |
| Playlist files | ✅ | ❌ Use `--filter` instead |
| Custom `ITestAdapter` extensions | ✅ | Must port to MTP extensions |

---

## Phase 3 — Azure DevOps pipeline changes

### 3a. Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
Before:
```yaml
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testSelector: 'testAssemblies'
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true
    resultsFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults'
```

After:
```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run tests (MTP)'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --no-build
      --
      --report-trx --report-trx-filename $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/results.trx
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
      --coverage-output $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml
```

### 3b. Publish test results
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish TRX results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'   # TRX format is still "VSTest" in this task
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 3c. Publish code coverage
```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish code coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

---

## Phase 4 — Roll out to remaining projects
Repeat Phases 1-2 for the other four test projects. If using `Directory.Build.props`,
centralize shared settings:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props (at solution root) -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```
Then tag each test csproj:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---

## Phase 5 — Cleanup
- Remove any leftover `.runsettings` entries that only configured VSTest collectors
  (MTP uses its own extension model).
- Delete `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `MSTest.TestAdapter` from `Directory.Packages.props`
  / central package management if present.
- Update developer docs / README with new local test commands.

---

## Risks & Notes
1. **`.runsettings` compatibility** — MTP accepts runsettings via `--settings`, but
   only MSTest-recognized settings are honored. VSTest-only adapter settings are
   ignored silently. Audit your runsettings file.
2. **Parallel test execution** — MTP runs each test project as its own process.
   Cross-project parallelism is handled by `dotnet test`. Intra-project parallelism
   is controlled by `[assembly: Parallelize]` or runsettings.
3. **Breaking change: OutputType Exe** — if any test project is referenced by another
   project as a library, you'll need to restructure. Test projects shouldn't be
   referenced by non-test projects.
4. **Minimum SDK** — .NET 8.0.400+ recommended for best MTP support via `dotnet test`.
   Verify your build agent SDK version.

---

## TODOs
1. Pilot: migrate first test project (project-file changes)
2. Verify locally: build, run, TRX, coverage
3. Update Azure DevOps pipeline (replace VSTest@3)
4. Verify pipeline: test results tab, coverage tab
5. Migrate remaining 4 test projects
6. Centralize via Directory.Build.props
7. Cleanup: remove dead packages, update docs
8. Audit .runsettings for VSTest-only settings

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Current State
- 5 MSTest test projects
- MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0 / MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0 / Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
- Azure DevOps CI with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage
- .NET 8 SDK

## Approach
Migrate each test project to the MTP runner built into MSTest 3.8+, replace the
VSTest@3 pipeline task with `dotnet test`, and wire up TRX + code-coverage via
MTP extension packages. Migrate one project first as a pilot, then roll out to the
remaining four.

---

## Phase 1 — Project-file changes (per test project)

### 1a. Remove VSTest-era packages
Remove from each `.csproj`:
```xml
<!-- DELETE these two PackageReferences -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
```
Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` (or upgrade it — see 1b).

### 1b. Enable the MTP runner
Add two properties and the runner meta-package:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <!-- Keeps dotnet test working alongside direct exe invocation -->
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```
`EnableMSTestRunner` bundles the MTP entry point into the test project.
`OutputType` must be `Exe` so the project compiles to a self-contained test host.

### 1c. Add MTP extension packages for TRX + coverage
```xml
<!-- TRX report generation (replaces VSTest's built-in TRX) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage via Microsoft's native collector -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```
> Version numbers above are latest stable as of May 2025 — check NuGet for updates.

### 1d. (Alternative) Use the MSTest SDK for a leaner csproj
Instead of steps 1a-1c you can switch the project SDK, which handles everything
automatically:
```xml
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```
The `MSTest.Sdk` already sets `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType`, and pulls in
the runner, framework, TRX, and code-coverage extension packages. This is the
cleanest option if your csproj files are straightforward.

---

## Phase 2 — Local verification

### 2a. Build & run locally
```bash
dotnet build MyTests.csproj
dotnet test MyTests.csproj          # uses MTP via dotnet-test bridge
./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyTests          # direct exe invocation (also works)
```

### 2b. Verify TRX output
```bash
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --report-trx
# TRX file written to TestResults/ by default
```

### 2c. Verify code coverage
```bash
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
# Produces coverage.cobertura.xml in TestResults/
```

### 2d. Check for incompatible features
| Feature | VSTest | MTP |
|---|---|---|
| `[DataRow]`, `[DynamicData]`, `[DataSource]` | ✅ | ✅ |
| `.runsettings` | Passed via `--settings` | Passed via `--settings` (same) |
| Ordered tests (`.orderedtest`) | ✅ | ❌ Not supported — refactor |
| Playlist files | ✅ | ❌ Use `--filter` instead |
| Custom `ITestAdapter` extensions | ✅ | Must port to MTP extensions |

---

## Phase 3 — Azure DevOps pipeline changes

### 3a. Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`
Before:
```yaml
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testSelector: 'testAssemblies'
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true
    resultsFolder: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults'
```

After:
```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run tests (MTP)'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release
      --no-build
      --
      --report-trx --report-trx-filename $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/results.trx
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
      --coverage-output $(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/coverage.cobertura.xml
```

### 3b. Publish test results
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish TRX results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'   # TRX format is still "VSTest" in this task
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 3c. Publish code coverage
```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish code coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

---

## Phase 4 — Roll out to remaining projects
Repeat Phases 1-2 for the other four test projects. If using `Directory.Build.props`,
centralize shared settings:

```xml

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```
Then tag each test csproj:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>
</PropertyGroup>
```

---

## Phase 5 — Cleanup
- Remove any leftover `.runsettings` entries that only configured VSTest collectors
  (MTP uses its own extension model).
- Delete `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `MSTest.TestAdapter` from `Directory.Packages.props`
  / central package management if present.
- Update developer docs / README with new local test commands.

---

3. **Breaking change: OutputType Exe** — if any test project is referenced by another
   project as a library, you'll need to restructure. Test projects shouldn't be
   referenced by non-test projects.
4. **Minimum SDK** — .NET 8.0.400+ recommended for best MTP support via `dotnet test`.
   Verify your build agent SDK version.

---

3. Update Azure DevOps pipeline (replace VSTest@3)
4. Verify pipeline: test results tab, coverage tab

7. Cleanup: remove dead packages, update docs

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Current State
- 5 MSTest test projects
- MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0 / MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0 / Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
- Azure DevOps CI with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage
- .NET 8 SDK

## Approach
Migrate each test project to the MTP runner built into MSTest 3.8+, replace the
VSTest@3 pipeline task with `dotnet test`, and wire up TRX + code-coverage via
MTP extension packages. Migrate one project first as a pilot, then roll out to the
remaining four.

---

## Phase 1 — Project-file changes (per test project)

### 1a. Remove VSTest-era packages
Remove from each `.csproj`:
```xml
<!-- DELETE these two PackageReferences -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
<PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
```
Keep `MSTest.TestFramework`.

### 1b. Enable the MTP runner
Add/update properties:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
</PropertyGroup>
```
- `EnableMSTestRunner` — bundles the MTP entry point.
- `OutputType Exe` — required so the project compiles to a runnable test host.
- `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` — keeps `dotnet test` working via the bridge.

### 1c. Add MTP extension packages for TRX + coverage
```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
```

### 1d. (Alternative) Use the MSTest SDK for a leaner csproj
```xml
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```
`MSTest.Sdk` sets `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType`, and pulls in runner,
framework, TRX, and coverage packages automatically. Cleanest option if your
csproj files are straightforward.

---

## Phase 2 — Local verification

```bash
# Build & run
dotnet test MyTests.csproj

# Verify TRX
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --report-trx

# Verify coverage
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

### Incompatible features to watch for
| Feature | MTP status |
|---|---|
| Ordered tests (`.orderedtest`) | ❌ Not supported — refactor |
| Playlist files | ❌ Use `--filter` instead |
| Custom `ITestAdapter` extensions | Must port to MTP extensions |

---

## Phase 3 — Azure DevOps pipeline changes

### 3a. Replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2
```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run tests (MTP)'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release --no-build
      -- --report-trx
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

### 3b. Publish results
```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '**/TestResults/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '**/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

---

## Phase 4 — Roll out & centralize
Centralize in `Directory.Build.props`:
```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

## Phase 5 — Cleanup
- Remove `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `MSTest.TestAdapter` from all package management.
- Audit `.runsettings` for VSTest-only collector settings (silently ignored by MTP).
- Update developer docs with new local test commands.

---

## Risks
1. **`.runsettings`** — only MSTest-recognized settings honored; VSTest adapter settings ignored.
2. **OutputType Exe** — breaks if test projects are referenced as libraries by other projects.
3. **Minimum SDK** — need .NET 8.0.400+ for full MTP + `dotnet test` support.
4. **Parallelism** — each test project runs as its own process; intra-project parallelism via `[assembly: Parallelize]`.

## TODOs

3. Update Azure DevOps pipeline
4. Verify pipeline: test results + coverage tabs
5. Migrate remaining 4 projects
6. Centralize via Directory.Build.props
7. Cleanup dead packages + update docs
8. Audit .runsettings

3. **Add** these properties:
   ```xml
   <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
   <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
   <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
   ```
4. **Add** extension packages:
   ```xml
   <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />
   <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.2" />
   ```

**Alternatively**, replace the entire csproj SDK with `MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0` which handles all of the above automatically.

### Phase 2 — Local verification
```bash
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --report-trx                          # verify TRX
dotnet test MyTests.csproj -- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura  # verify coverage
```

### Phase 3 — Azure DevOps pipeline
Replace `VSTest@3` with:
```yaml
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  inputs:
    command: test
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: '--configuration Release --no-build -- --report-trx --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura'

- task: PublishTestResults@2
  condition: always()
  inputs: { testResultsFormat: VSTest, testResultsFiles: '**/TestResults/**/*.trx', mergeTestResults: true }

- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  condition: always()
  inputs: { summaryFileLocation: '**/TestResults/**/coverage.cobertura.xml' }
```

### Phase 4 — Roll out & centralize
Migrate remaining 4 projects; centralize settings in `Directory.Build.props` using `<IsTestProject>true</IsTestProject>` conditions.

### Phase 5 — Cleanup
Remove dead packages, audit `.runsettings` for VSTest-only settings, update docs.

### Key risks
- **`.runsettings`**: VSTest adapter settings are silently ignored — audit yours
- **`OutputType Exe`**: breaks if test projects are referenced as libraries
- **SDK version**: need **.NET 8.0.400+** on build agents for full MTP support
- **Ordered tests / playlists**: not supported in MTP — refactor to `--filter`

Recommend piloting one project end-to-end before rolling out to all five.
